       d.name AS department_name,
       SUM(CASE WHEN ce.would_recommend THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS recommended_count,
       COUNT(ce.id) AS total_evaluations,
       100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN ce.would_recommend THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / NULLIF(COUNT(ce.id), 0) AS percent_recommended
FROM public.course_evaluations ce
JOIN public.enrollments e ON ce.enrollment_id = e.id
JOIN public.course_sections cs ON e.course_section_id = cs.id
JOIN public.courses c ON cs.course_id = c.id
JOIN public.departments d ON c.department_id = d.id
GROUP BY d.id, d.name

data.department_name: Wydział Informatyki i Nauki o Materiałach

visualization.title: Percentage of students who would recommend courses by department

resultRaw.sql:
SELECT d.id AS department_id,
       d.name AS department_name,
       SUM(CASE WHEN ce.would_recommend THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS recommended_count,
       COUNT(ce.id) AS total_evaluations,
       100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN ce.would_recommend THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / NULLIF(COUNT(ce.id), 0) AS percent_recommended
FROM public.course_evaluations ce
JOIN public.enrollments e ON ce.enrollment_id = e.id
JOIN public.course_sections cs ON e.course_section_id = cs.id
JOIN public.courses c ON cs.course_id = c.id
JOIN public.departments d ON c.department_id = d.id
GROUP BY d.id, d.name
ORDER BY percent_recommended DESC, department_name ASC;

sample.department_name: Wydział Informatyki i Nauki o Materiałach

issues: department_id is present in the query output but not shown in the chart or tooltip — consider including it for unambiguous identification.
Vega-Lite x.sort uses percent_recommended descending but does not encode the SQL's secondary ordering by department_name for tie-breaks; ordering for ties may differ from the SQL ORDER BY.

Departments with zero evaluations are excluded by the aggregation (percent_recommended would be NULL); this matches the SQL but is a potential data-coverage concern if you expected to display departments with zero responses.

data.department_name: Wydział Informatyki i Nauki o Materiałach

visualization.title: Percentage of students who would recommend courses by department

result.sql:
SELECT d.id AS department_id,
       d.name AS department_name,
       SUM(CASE WHEN ce.would_recommend THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS recommended_count,
       COUNT(ce.id) AS total_evaluations,
       100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN ce.would_recommend THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / NULLIF(COUNT(ce.id), 0) AS percent_recommended
FROM public.course_evaluations ce
JOIN public.enrollments e ON ce.enrollment_id = e.id
JOIN public.course_sections cs ON e.course_section_id = cs.id
JOIN public.courses c ON cs.course_id = c.id
JOIN public.departments d ON c.department_id = d.id
GROUP BY d.id, d.name
ORDER BY percent_recommended DESC, department_name ASC;

sample.department_name: Wydział Informatyki i Nauki o Materiałach

issues: department_id is present in the query output but not shown in the chart or tooltip — consider including it for unambiguous identification.
Vega-Lite x.sort uses percent_recommended descending but does not encode the SQL's secondary ordering by department_name for tie-breaks; ordering for ties may differ from the SQL ORDER BY.

Departments with zero evaluations are excluded by the aggregation (percent_recommended would be NULL); this matches the SQL but is a potential data-coverage concern if you expected to display departments with zero responses.
